topic: llama-stack: Meta's Unified Deployment Stack for Llama 4 Models

key_points: Comprehensive practical tutorial with code examples for inference, RAG, and agents
Covers installation via pip and uv, server setup, and client SDK usage
Explains Llama Stack's OpenAI-compatible API surface
Shows how to swap providers without changing application code
Step-by-step guide specific to AMD Instinct GPU deployment
Covers ROCm-based inference with vLLM as provider
Demonstrates end-to-end pipeline from model download to API serving
Performance benchmarks on AMD hardware
Developer-focused getting started overview
Covers YAML configuration and provider setup
Explains Llama Stack's composable building block philosophy
Practical code snippets for client SDK usage
Official detailed tutorial from the llama-stack GitHub Pages
Covers Docker and uv-based installation methods
Explains starter distribution and configuration file generation
Lists all supported inference providers and their trade-offs
Enterprise safety focus: PromptGuard and Llama Guard integration
How to implement content moderation and violation level configuration
Red Hat's production deployment patterns for Llama Stack
Use of Safety API shields in agentic workflows

stats.fact: Llama models have been downloaded over 1.2 billion times by April 2025, making it the most widely adopted open-weight AI model family in the world
Over 50% of Fortune 500 companies piloted Llama solutions by March 2025
Projected enterprise spending on Llama solutions reached $2.5 billion by 2026
Llama 4 Scout has 17 billion active parameters with 16 experts and fits in a single NVIDIA H100 GPU, beating all previous generation Llama models
Llama 4 Maverick uses 17 billion active parameters with 128 experts and outperforms GPT-4o and Gemini 2.0 Flash across broad benchmarks
vLLM version 0.6.0 achieved 2.7x higher throughput and 5x faster time-per-output-token on Llama 8B compared to version 0.5.3, a key inference provider for Llama Stack
PromptGuard runs as a lightweight microservice with only 86 million parameters, enabling fast CPU-based safety classification without a GPU
vLLM shows roughly 2.3x higher throughput than Ollama under 8 concurrent users, making provider choice critical for production Llama Stack deployments

angles: Llama Stack as a 'run-anywhere' standardized contract: deploy on laptop, datacenter, or cloud with the same application code
Multi-provider flexibility without vendor lock-in — swap between Ollama, vLLM, TGI, AWS Bedrock, or Azure OpenAI by changing only config
Enterprise safety built-in from day one: PromptGuard + Llama Guard shields for production-grade content moderation
OpenAI API compatibility means drop-in replacement for existing OpenAI-based apps without code changes
MCP (Model Context Protocol) support enables reusing existing agent tools and workflows directly in Llama Stack
Step-by-step beginner guide: from pip install to running your first RAG pipeline in under 30 minutes
Llama 4 multimodal capabilities (Scout + Maverick) unlocked through Llama Stack's unified inference API
